bead.title: Dashboard UI: cross-project beads view (combined ready/in-progress/closed across all projects)
bead.description:
Same situational-awareness gap as the workers view: when N projects are in flight, the operator currently can only see one project's bead queue at a time via /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads. Add a node-level combined beads view that merges all projects' queues into one filterable table.

Backing data: needs a new endpoint OR client-side merge. The cleanest design is server-side aggregation since clients shouldn't fan out N requests:
- New endpoint GET /api/beads (node-wide, no project scope)
- Returns: array of beads with an extra 'project_id' field on each entry
- Supports the same query params as /api/projects/:project/beads (?status=, ?ready=, ?label=, ?limit=)
- Walks the project registry from FEAT-020 and concatenates each project's bead store

UI requirements:
- Route: /nodes/:nodeId/beads (combined; the existing /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/beads stays)
- Adds 'Project' column showing project short name
- Filter chips for status (open/in_progress/closed) and project (multi-select)
- Filter state encoded in URL query params so views are bookmarkable
- Auto-refresh every ~10s (slower than workers since beads change less frequently)

Delete-aware: when a project is removed from the registry its beads disappear from the combined view on the next refresh.
bead.acceptance: Backend Go test in cli/internal/server/server_test.go: seeds two projects via state.RegisterProject, each with three beads (open, in_progress, closed), calls GET /api/beads, asserts (1) response contains all six beads, (2) each bead has project_id matching its source project, (3) ?status=open returns only the two open beads, (4) ?project_id=<id> returns only that project's beads. Playwright test loads /nodes/:nodeId/beads, asserts the table contains beads from at least two distinct projects with the Project column populated, asserts filter chip status=open hides closed/in_progress rows, asserts a project filter chip narrows the table to one project's beads, asserts URL query params reflect the filter state.
